problem_statement: Implement merge_intervals(intervals, merge_touching=True) in py/intervals.py. Given a list of closed numeric (start, end) tuples in any order, return a new ascending-sorted list where every group of overlapping intervals is collapsed into one spanning interval. The merge_touching flag controls the boundary policy: when True (default), intervals that merely touch — one ending exactly where the next starts — also merge; when False they stay separate. Raise ValueError if any interval has start greater than end, never modify the input list, and return [] for empty input. Verify with python3 -m pytest -q tests/py/test_intervals*.py.
